cards.definition: a period of ten years
cards.example: The firm has grown steadily over the past decade.

cards.definition: to officially announce or state something
cards.example: Travelers must declare any goods purchased abroad.

cards.definition: to decrease or politely refuse
cards.example: Sales declined by twelve percent in the third quarter.

cards.definition: to subtract an amount from a total
cards.example: The company will deduct travel expenses from the reimbursement.

cards.definition: a flaw or imperfection in a product
cards.example: Quality inspectors found a defect in the circuit board.

cards.definition: a lack or shortage of something needed
cards.example: The audit revealed a deficiency in the current safety procedures.

cards.definition: to assign responsibility or tasks to another person
cards.example: Managers should delegate routine tasks to junior staff.

cards.definition: to show how something works or is done
cards.example: The sales representative will demonstrate the new software features.

cards.definition: a division within an organization
cards.example: Please forward the invoice to the accounting department.

cards.definition: an initial partial payment to secure a purchase
cards.example: A deposit of five hundred dollars is required to reserve the venue.

cards.definition: to officially assign a role or purpose
cards.example: The parking lot was designated for employee use only.

cards.definition: to create or improve something over time
cards.example: The firm plans to develop a new line of eco-friendly products.

cards.definition: a certificate awarded upon completing a course of study
cards.example: Applicants must hold a diploma in business administration.

cards.definition: a listing of names, addresses, or information
cards.example: You can find her extension number in the company directory.

cards.definition: to stop producing or offering something
cards.example: The manufacturer decided to discontinue the older model.

cards.definition: a reduction in the usual price
cards.example: Members receive a ten-percent discount on all purchases.

cards.definition: to terminate someone's employment
cards.example: The employee was dismissed for repeated policy violations.

cards.definition: to send goods or personnel to a destination
cards.example: The warehouse will dispatch your order within two business days.

cards.definition: able to withstand wear and last a long time
cards.example: These durable containers are ideal for overseas shipping.

cards.definition: achieving results with minimal waste of time or resources
cards.example: The new workflow is far more efficient than the old process.

cards.definition: meeting the requirements to participate or receive
cards.example: Full-time employees are eligible for health insurance benefits.

cards.definition: to give special importance to something
cards.example: The director emphasized the need for stricter quality controls.

cards.definition: to hire someone for paid work
cards.example: The factory employs over three hundred workers.

cards.definition: to unexpectedly come across a situation or problem
cards.example: Customers may encounter brief delays during the system upgrade.

cards.definition: to publicly support or approve something
cards.example: Several industry leaders endorsed the new safety regulations.

cards.definition: to improve the quality or value of something
cards.example: Renovations were made to enhance the hotel's guest experience.

cards.definition: to register or sign up for a program
cards.example: Employees can enroll in the professional development course online.

cards.definition: a business organization or venture
cards.example: The grant supports small enterprises in the technology sector.

cards.definition: provided with the necessary tools or features
cards.example: Each conference room is equipped with a projector and microphone.

cards.definition: to set up or create something formally
cards.example: The company was established in nineteen ninety-five.

cards.definition: to assess the quality or value of something
cards.example: Supervisors evaluate employee performance twice a year.

cards.definition: to go beyond a set limit or expectation
cards.example: Revenue this quarter exceeded our original projections.

cards.definition: limited to a particular group or available only to few
cards.example: Premium members enjoy exclusive access to the executive lounge.

cards.definition: freed from a rule or obligation that applies to others
cards.example: Non-profit organizations are exempt from certain taxes.

cards.definition: the amount of money spent on something
cards.example: Annual expenditure on marketing increased by fifteen percent.

cards.definition: to reach the end of a period of validity
cards.example: The warranty on this appliance will expire next month.

cards.definition: additional time or an extra part added to something
cards.example: The client requested a two-week extension on the project deadline.

cards.definition: to ensure that rules or laws are obeyed
cards.example: Security staff will enforce the new visitor identification policy.

cards.definition: to make a process easier or more efficient
cards.example: The new software will facilitate online collaboration among teams.

cards.definition: to make someone aware of or accustomed to something
cards.example: New hires should familiarize themselves with the employee handbook.

cards.definition: possible and practical to achieve
cards.example: The board determined that expanding overseas was financially feasible.

cards.definition: to complete the last details of a plan or agreement
cards.example: We need to finalize the contract before the end of the week.

cards.definition: relating to government or company finances
cards.example: The fiscal year ends on March thirty-first.

cards.definition: to rise and fall irregularly
cards.example: Raw material prices tend to fluctuate throughout the year.

cards.definition: an irregular rise and fall in amount or level
cards.example: Currency fluctuation affected our profit margins this quarter.

cards.definition: a prediction of future trends or conditions
cards.example: The sales forecast for next quarter looks very promising.

cards.definition: to lose a right or possession as a penalty
cards.example: Late applicants will forfeit their registration deposit.

cards.definition: about to happen or appear in the near future
cards.example: Details about the forthcoming merger will be announced Friday.

cards.definition: to send a message or document on to another person
cards.example: Please forward the meeting agenda to all department heads.

cards.definition: easily broken or damaged
cards.example: Fragile items must be packed with extra protective material.

cards.definition: a license to operate a business under a brand name
cards.example: She opened a franchise of the popular coffee chain downtown.

cards.definition: goods transported in bulk by truck, train, or ship
cards.example: The freight arrived at the port two days ahead of schedule.

cards.definition: occurring often or at short intervals
cards.example: The shuttle bus runs frequently between the airport and the hotel.

cards.definition: to carry out or complete a task or order
cards.example: The warehouse is working overtime to fulfill holiday orders.

cards.definition: to provide money for a project or organization
cards.example: The government will fund research into renewable energy sources.

cards.definition: to supply or provide something needed
cards.example: Applicants are asked to furnish two professional references.

cards.definition: to create or devise a plan or strategy
cards.example: The committee will formulate a policy on remote work arrangements.

cards.definition: to measure or assess the level of something
cards.example: The survey was designed to gauge customer satisfaction levels.

cards.definition: to produce or create something
cards.example: The marketing campaign generated significant interest from investors.

cards.definition: larger or more than expected; willing to give
cards.example: The company offers a generous benefits package to all employees.

cards.definition: truly what it is claimed to be; authentic
cards.example: Please verify that the signature on the contract is genuine.

cards.definition: a quick or brief look at something
cards.example: At first glance, the quarterly report showed positive trends.

cards.definition: a brief or partial view of something
cards.example: The preview gave attendees a glimpse of the upcoming product line.

cards.definition: happening slowly over a period of time
cards.example: The company gradually expanded into international markets.

cards.definition: a sum of money given for a specific purpose
cards.example: The startup received a government grant for product development.

cards.definition: to understand something fully
cards.example: New employees need time to grasp the company procedures.

cards.definition: a feeling of thankfulness and appreciation
cards.example: The director expressed gratitude to the volunteers at the event.

cards.definition: a formal complaint about unfair treatment
cards.example: Employees may file a grievance through the human resources office.

cards.definition: innovative and introducing new ideas or methods
cards.example: The lab announced a groundbreaking discovery in battery technology.

cards.definition: a formal promise that a product or service will perform
cards.example: All appliances come with a two-year manufacturer guarantee.

cards.definition: recommended rules or instructions for a procedure
cards.example: Please review the safety guidelines before entering the facility.

cards.definition: an item of clothing
cards.example: The factory produces garments for several well-known fashion brands.

cards.definition: a room or building for displaying works of art
cards.example: The corporate lobby features a gallery of local artwork.

cards.definition: an assembly of people for a particular purpose
cards.example: The annual gathering of shareholders will be held next Tuesday.

cards.definition: the process of business becoming international in scope
cards.example: Globalization has opened new markets for domestic manufacturers.
